hero.question: Will Connecticut win the 2026 NCAA Tournament?
hero.prob_take: At 13.9%, the crowd has essentially priced Connecticut out of contention, treating them like a program with no realistic path to a championship in two years. Dan Hurley's roster is young but tournament-talented, and the gap between 13.9% and 18-22% (where a perennial tournament team should sit) is wide enough for a sharp to take a flyer, especially if UConn makes a late-season tourney run this year as proof of concept.

daily_take.headline: Connecticut's Tournament Odds Stuck in Neutral While Arizona Catches Fire
daily_take.deck: Connecticut sits at 13.9% to win the 2026 NCAA Tournament, up a whisper on flat volume, while Arizona jumped 2.2 points to 32.6% on $1.8M in action. The market is pricing Connecticut as a mid-tier tournament team, but with Dan Hurley rebuilding and zero NBA talent on the roster, the Huskies look overvalued. Arizona's move reflects real roster momentum. Short Connecticut and ride Arizona's momentum instead.
